title: Xylanthemum rupestre

It belongs to the genus Xylanthemum, a group of plants that are often found in mountainous regions. Xylanthemum rupestre is native to the mountainous areas of China. intro: Xylanthemum rupestre, also known as Korean sage, is a perennial plant in the daisy family that is native to East Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce stress and improve cognitive function. The bioactive constituents of Xylanthemum rupestre include triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Xylanthemum rupestre is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into traditional herbal remedies, such as soups and salves, to promote relaxation and improve overall well-being.
